title: neumannia minima
intro: Neumannia minima, also known as the Indian pennywort or lesser duckweed, is a small, aquatic plant that belongs to the family Linderniaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions, such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. Neumannia minima can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional remedies and modern supplements to leverage its medicinal properties.
